xEnrich 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Run enrichment analysis: hypergeometric test or GSEA-like scoring

Source: R/run_enrichment.R
run_enrichment.Rd

A unified interface for two enrichment approaches sharing a common output structure. The active method is controlled by method; certain arguments are only meaningful for one method (see Input contracts below).

run_enrichment(
  gene_list,
  universe = NULL,
  method = c("hypergeometric", "gsea"),
  gene_set = NULL,
  gene_stats = NULL,
  gsea_weight = 1,
  n_perm = 1000L,
  alternative = c("greater", "less", "two.sided"),
  adaptive = FALSE,
  adaptive_mode = c("pvalue", "decision", "refine"),
  eps = 0.01,
  alpha = 0.05,
  seed = NULL,
  keep_trace = FALSE
)

Arguments

gene_list

Character vector of gene symbols defining the gene set under test. For hypergeometric: the query set. For GSEA: the pathway whose enrichment is being assessed in the gene_stats ranking.

universe

Character vector of background genes. For hypergeometric, defaults to union(gene_list, gene_set). For GSEA, defaults to names(gene_stats).

method

Character. "hypergeometric" (default) or "gsea".

gene_set

Character vector. The pathway / biological set for the hypergeometric test. Ignored for GSEA.

gene_stats

Named numeric vector. Per-gene scores defining the ranked list for GSEA (e.g. log fold-change, t-statistic). Names are gene symbols. Ignored for hypergeometric.

gsea_weight

Numeric. Exponent for weighting hits by |gene_stats|^gsea_weight. Default 1. Use 0 for the classic unweighted KS statistic.

n_perm

Integer. Permutations for the GSEA null. Default 1000. Ignored for hypergeometric.

alternative

Character. "greater" (default), "less", or "two.sided". For hypergeometric with "two.sided", delegates to stats::fisher.test().

adaptive

Logical. Use adaptive permutation stopping? Default FALSE. GSEA only; ignored with a warning for hypergeometric.

adaptive_mode

Character. "pvalue" (default), "decision", or "refine". See Adaptive permutation modes. Ignored when adaptive = FALSE.

eps

Numeric. RSE threshold for adaptive_mode = "pvalue". Default 0.01.

alpha

Numeric. Significance level for CI-based decisions and stability_flag. Default 0.05.

seed

Optional integer. Passed to set.seed() before any stochastic computation.

keep_trace

Logical. If TRUE, attach per-batch diagnostic traces to the inference slot. Default FALSE. Ignored for hypergeometric.

Value

A named list with:

method

Method used.

p_value

Final p-value.

overlap

Number of query genes found in the set / ranking.

input_set_size

Size of gene_list after universe intersection.

universe_size

Size of universe.

fold_change

(Hypergeometric only.) Observed / expected overlap ratio.

group_set

(Hypergeometric only.) Size of gene_set in universe.

enrichment_score

(GSEA only.) Signed ES.

universe_size_used

(GSEA only.) Genes retained after NA removal and universe intersection.

leading_edge

(GSEA only.) Genes driving the ES peak.

inference

(GSEA only.) List of inference diagnostics. NULL for hypergeometric.

Input contracts

Hypergeometric (method = "hypergeometric")

Requires gene_set (the pathway/biological set) and gene_list (the query). universe defaults to the union of both. gene_stats, gsea_weight, n_perm, adaptive, adaptive_mode, eps, and keep_trace are ignored with a warning.

GSEA (method = "gsea")

Requires gene_stats (named numeric vector of per-gene scores — the ranked list) and gene_list (the gene set being tested). universe defaults to names(gene_stats). gene_set is ignored.

Terminology note

In this function, gene_list is the gene set (pathway) being tested for enrichment, and gene_stats supplies the ranked list of all genes. This is the reverse of the naming convention in some tools (e.g. fgsea). See the gene_stats argument description.

GSEA weighting

Gene weights are computed as |gene_stats|^gsea_weight. Taking the absolute value means genes with large negative scores (e.g. strongly down-regulated) receive the same weight magnitude as genes with large positive scores. This is intentional and consistent with the original weighted GSEA formulation (Subramanian et al. 2005). Set gsea_weight = 0 for the unweighted (classic KS) statistic.

Adaptive permutation modes

"pvalue"

Draws batches until RSE (relative standard error of p-hat) < eps. Best when an accurate p-value is needed.

"decision"

Draws batches until the Wilson CI for p-hat lies entirely above or below alpha. Best when only the sig/nonsig decision matters.

"refine"

Runs a fixed n_perm first; if the result is borderline, switches to decision-mode refinement. Best for computational efficiency across many gene sets.

References

Subramanian, A. et al. (2005). Gene set enrichment analysis. PNAS, 102(43), 15545–15550.

See also

run_info_enrichment() for information-theoretic scoring, run_redundancy_selection() for post-enrichment redundancy reduction.

Examples

# --- Hypergeometric ---
universe  <- paste0("G", 1:500)
gene_list <- sample(universe, 50)
gene_set  <- sample(universe, 40)

run_enrichment(
  gene_list = gene_list,
  universe  = universe,
  method    = "hypergeometric",
  gene_set  = gene_set
)
#> $method
#> [1] "hypergeometric"
#> 
#> $p_value
#> [1] 0.1987283
#> 
#> $overlap
#> [1] 6
#> 
#> $input_set_size
#> [1] 50
#> 
#> $group_set
#> [1] 40
#> 
#> $universe_size
#> [1] 500
#> 
#> $fold_change
#> [1] 1.5
#> 
#> $inference
#> NULL
#> 

# --- GSEA, fixed permutations ---
gene_stats        <- setNames(rnorm(500), universe)
gene_list_gsea    <- sample(universe, 40)

run_enrichment(
  gene_list  = gene_list_gsea,
  method     = "gsea",
  gene_stats = gene_stats,
  n_perm     = 500,
  seed       = 1
)
#> $method
#> [1] "gsea"
#> 
#> $enrichment_score
#> [1] -0.2867898
#> 
#> $p_value
#> [1] 0.8922156
#> 
#> $overlap
#> [1] 40
#> 
#> $input_set_size
#> [1] 40
#> 
#> $universe_size
#> [1] 500
#> 
#> $universe_size_used
#> [1] 500
#> 
#> $leading_edge
#> [1] "G293" "G372" "G411" "G238" "G123" "G357" "G177" "G350"
#> 
#> $inference
#> $inference$method
#> [1] "fixed"
#> 
#> $inference$fixed_k
#> [1] 446
#> 
#> $inference$fixed_B
#> [1] 500
#> 
#> $inference$res
#> $inference$res$k_extreme
#> [1] 446
#> 
#> $inference$res$n_perm
#> [1] 500
#> 
#> $inference$res$p_hat
#> [1] 0.8922156
#> 
#> $inference$res$p_ci_low
#> [1] 0.862016
#> 
#> $inference$res$p_ci_high
#> [1] 0.9164463
#> 
#> $inference$res$mc_se
#> [1] 0.01386845
#> 
#> $inference$res$decision_alpha
#> [1] "nonsig"
#> 
#> $inference$res$stability_flag
#> [1] FALSE
#> 
#> 
#> 

# --- GSEA, adaptive (refine mode) ---
run_enrichment(
  gene_list     = gene_list_gsea,
  method        = "gsea",
  gene_stats    = gene_stats,
  n_perm        = 500,
  adaptive      = TRUE,
  adaptive_mode = "refine",
  seed          = 1
)
#> $method
#> [1] "gsea"
#> 
#> $enrichment_score
#> [1] -0.2867898
#> 
#> $p_value
#> [1] 0.8922156
#> 
#> $overlap
#> [1] 40
#> 
#> $input_set_size
#> [1] 40
#> 
#> $universe_size
#> [1] 500
#> 
#> $universe_size_used
#> [1] 500
#> 
#> $leading_edge
#> [1] "G293" "G372" "G411" "G238" "G123" "G357" "G177" "G350"
#> 
#> $inference
#> $inference$method
#> [1] "fixed_not_refined"
#> 
#> $inference$fixed_k
#> [1] 446
#> 
#> $inference$fixed_B
#> [1] 500
#> 
#> $inference$res
#> $inference$res$k_extreme
#> [1] 446
#> 
#> $inference$res$n_perm
#> [1] 500
#> 
#> $inference$res$p_hat
#> [1] 0.8922156
#> 
#> $inference$res$p_ci_low
#> [1] 0.862016
#> 
#> $inference$res$p_ci_high
#> [1] 0.9164463
#> 
#> $inference$res$mc_se
#> [1] 0.01386845
#> 
#> $inference$res$decision_alpha
#> [1] "nonsig"
#> 
#> $inference$res$stability_flag
#> [1] FALSE
#> 
#> 
#>
